lever.name: parallel B-panel packing
lever.description: split the large B-panel packing copy across disjoint NR-panel ranges using the existing bounded matmul thread budget

isomorphism.packed_bytes: identical panel-major [panel][k][NR] bytes; direct layout test now exercises the parallel path
isomorphism.nan_signed_zero_surface: to_bits contract preserved in focused tests

proof.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib matmul_2d -- --nocapture

benchmark.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/matmul_2d_2048x2048x2048_f64 --sample-size 10 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

validation.cargo_check: RCH ts1 cargo check -p fj-lax --all-targets passed
validation.clippy: RCH ts1 cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings passed
validation.ubs: ubs crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_contraction.rs exited 0; file-wide pre-existing warning inventory remains

decision.reason: same-worker median speedup 1.4260x and conservative speedup 1.3496x clear Score >= 2.0

next_route: Reprofile after landing; if GEMM remains top, avoid B-pack layout retuning and look for a deeper exact-order parallel/data-motion primitive or unlock FP-tolerant recursive kernels through explicit contract work.
